description_long: This chapel is a small, rectangular religious building with white plastered walls and a red-tiled gabled roof, set atop a grassy and rocky terrain base. The structure features minimal fenestration, with a few small, rectangular windows along the sides and a single, more ornate window above the main entrance. The front facade is distinguished by decorative architectural elements, including curved gables and finials, and a prominent entrance door. On one end, a modest bell tower or steeple rises, adorned with classical detailing and a small spire. The building sits on a slightly elevated stone foundation, giving it a sense of prominence in its setting. The overall design is simple yet elegant, reflecting traditional European chapel architecture.
description: A small white chapel with a red-tiled roof, decorative front facade, and a modest bell tower, set on a grassy, rocky base. The structure features minimal windows and classical architectural details.
description_view_0: Side view showing the long, plain white wall with no windows and the red-tiled roof, set on a grassy and rocky base.
description_view_1: Front view highlighting the main entrance door, a decorative window above it, and ornate gable details with finials.
description_view_2: Opposite side view with several small windows and a small arched opening, as well as the base of the bell tower.
description_view_3: Rear view showing the simple, unadorned back wall and the full length of the red-tiled roof.
attribute_steeple_or_bell_tower: Present; a small, square bell tower with classical detailing and a spire is located at one end of the chapel, rising above the roofline.
attribute_entrance_door_design: The main entrance door is rectangular, made of wood, and painted brown, set within a simple stone or plaster frame. Above the door is a decorative window, and the facade features curved gables and finials for added ornamentation.
